commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3544e599952bd1d2e5fda9e1c47b0271a5cb1689)
commands.review_summary:
The PR rejects Z.AI onboarding command text during API-key setup, surfaces polluted Z.AI static profile hints through doctor, adds `issueKind` to the provider auth doctor context, and adds Z.AI/Anthropic/OpenAI regression tests.

PR surface: Source +83, Tests +191. Total +274 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can store non-empty Z.AI command text as `zai:default`, and current doctor auth health only reports OAuth/token issues. I did not run a live Z.AI credential repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the permanent plugin API shape for static auth-profile doctor hints before this can be safely repaired or merged.

target.title: fix(zai): reject onboarding command auth profiles
target.body:
﻿Related: #97313

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes the Z.AI command-string auth-profile pollution path where users could end up with `zai:default` storing onboarding command text such as `openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global` as the API key, causing provider calls to use that command string as the bearer token instead of a real key.

## Why This Change Was Made

The setup guard is owned by the Z.AI provider: interactive and non-interactive Z.AI API-key auth now reject onboarding command text before the value can be persisted.

The doctor path is split by ownership:

- Core doctor only performs generic static API-key profile surfacing.
- Static doctor surfacing marks provider hook calls with `issueKind: "static_api_key"`.
- OAuth repair hint calls use `issueKind: "oauth"`.
- Z.AI owns the provider-specific static predicate and recovery copy through `buildAuthDoctorHint`.
- OAuth-only sibling hooks such as Anthropic and OpenAI ignore static API-key doctor checks.

This addresses both ClawSweeper review rounds: Z.AI-specific command detection and wording no longer live in core doctor, and static API-key doctor surfacing no longer misuses OAuth-only provider hooks.

This PR does not change sub-agent fallback behavior or broader key-rotation semantics; those remain with the linked issue scope.

## User Impact

Users get an immediate setup error if they paste an OpenClaw onboarding command where a Z.AI API key is expected. Existing polluted `zai:default` profiles can be surfaced by doctor as `static [invalid_api_key]` with a provider-owned re-authentication hint, instead of repeatedly failing as an opaque Z.AI 401.

## Evidence

Red/green proof from the initial fix:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before the fix, the new regression tests failed because Z.AI returned a profile with `key: "openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global"` and doctor returned an empty hint. After the fix: 2 files passed, 19 tests passed.

Review-fix proof before the latest Windows runner hang:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before routing static Z.AI API-key profiles through `noteAuthProfileHealthForTarget`, the new regression failed with `Number of calls: 0`. After the fix, the focused suite passed in an earlier run: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed.

Combined focused proof before later Windows runner hangs:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Result: 3 files passed, 25 tests passed.

Fresh checks after guarding static API-key doctor hook calls:

```text
npx oxfmt --check extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts extensions/anthropic/register.runtime.ts extensions/anthropic/index.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-provider.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/plugins/types.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts
npx oxlint extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts extensions/anthropic/register.runtime.ts extensions/anthropic/index.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-provider.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/plugins/types.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
git diff --check
node --import tsx -e "await import('./src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts'); await import('./src/commands/doctor-auth.ts'); await import('./extensions/zai/index.ts'); await import('./extensions/anthropic/index.ts'); await import('./extensions/openai/openai-provider.ts'); console.log('imports passed');"
```

All exited 0.

Fresh provider-hook behavior checks:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import plugin from './extensions/zai/index.ts'; import { registerSingleProviderPlugin } from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/plugin-test-runtime'; const provider = await registerSingleProviderPlugin(plugin); const store = { version: 1, profiles: { 'zai:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'zai', key: 'openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global' } } }; const hint = provider.buildAuthDoctorHint?.({ provider: 'zai', profileId: 'zai:default', issueKind: 'static_api_key', store }); if (hint !== 'Z.AI auth profile zai:default contains an onboarding command instead of an API key. Re-authenticate with a real key: openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global.') throw new Error(String(hint)); if (provider.buildAuthDoctorHint?.({ provider: 'zai', profileId: 'zai:default', issueKind: 'oauth', store }) !== undefined) throw new Error('unexpected oauth hint'); console.log('zai static hook assertion passed');"
node --import tsx -e "import anthropicPlugin from './extensions/anthropic/index.ts'; import { registerSingleProviderPlugin } from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/plugin-test-runtime'; const provider = await registerSingleProviderPlugin(anthropicPlugin); const result = provider.buildAuthDoctorHint?.({ provider: 'anthropic', profileId: 'anthropic:default', issueKind: 'static_api_key', store: { version: 1, profiles: { 'anthropic:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'anthropic', key: 'sk-ant-api03-real' }, 'anthropic:user@example.test': { type: 'oauth', provider: 'anthropic', access: 'access', refresh: 'refresh', expires: Date.now() + 60000 } } } }); if (result !== undefined) throw new Error(String(result)); console.log('anthropic static hook guard passed');"
node --import tsx -e "import { buildOpenAIProvider } from './extensions/openai/openai-provider.ts'; const provider = buildOpenAIProvider(); const result = provider.buildAuthDoctorHint?.({ provider: 'openai', profileId: 'openai-codex:default', issueKind: 'static_api_key', store: { version: 1, profiles: { 'openai-codex:default': { type: 'api_key', provider: 'openai', key: 'sk-real' } } } }); if (result !== undefined) throw new Error(String(result)); console.log('openai static hook guard passed');"
```

All exited 0.

Current Windows proof gap:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

This did not complete on this Windows worktree; it was stopped after repeated no-output waits. Raw Vitest reruns also hung during Rolldown/Vitest initialization with repeated `[PLUGIN_TIMINGS]` warnings and no test failure summary. The focused suites above passed before this runner issue, and the fresh provider-hook assertions plus lint/format/import/surface checks cover the latest code movement.

Validation note: I do not have a live Z.AI / GLM-5.2 credential, so this PR proves the local auth-profile root cause with seeded/plugin/doctor-path evidence rather than a live network call.
target.author: lzy3538

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
